region_of_origin: United States (served in Singapore via Subway)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a class for submarine sandwich (FOODON_00002473) but no specific entry for the Italian BMT variant or a soup meal combo. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers sandwiches broadly; no specific code exists for Italian BMT or a sandwich-soup meal combo. Closest parent used.

main_ingredients: Italian bread (white or wheat sub roll)

serving.description: 1 meal (6-inch Italian BMT sub + 1 cup soup)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a 6-inch Italian B.M.T. submarine sandwich (approximately 280 g) paired with one cup of vegetable soup (approximately 170 g), as typically served in a Subway soup meal combo in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Italian B.M.T. 6-inch sub (USDA Branded Food) combined with Subway Vegetable Beef Soup / generic vegetable soup estimate from USDA SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central — Branded Foods and SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central Branded Food entry for Subway Italian B.M.T. 6-inch sub (FDC ID 2097512, per 100 g basis) combined proportionally with USDA SR Legacy vegetable soup (FDC ID 173170) to represent the full meal. Per-100 g values represent the weighted composite of the sub (~62% of meal weight) and soup (~38% of meal weight). No direct Singapore-specific entry exists in HPB Foodo for this branded combo meal. USDA Branded Food is the highest-priority approved source for this commercial product.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of bread-based mixed meals — white bread sub roll analogue; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Subway Italian B.M.T. Soup Meal. GI of 53 is estimated using the University of Sydney GI Database entry for a white-bread-based submarine sandwich with mixed fillings (GI ~53, low category), consistent with Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables. The soup component (vegetable soup, GI ~48) has minimal impact on the composite GI given its low carbohydrate contribution. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (53 × 83.25) / 100 ≈ 44.1, rounded to 44.0. Carbohydrate per serving = 18.5 g/100 g × 450 g = 83.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 2340 mg sodium per full meal serving (520 mg/100 g × 450 g), which exceeds the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg in a single meal
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal due to its very high sodium load from processed deli meats (pepperoni, salami, ham) and soup; if consumed, request no added salt, minimal sauce, and pair with a low-sodium beverage.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (GL ≈ 44) driven by the refined white bread sub roll and total carbohydrate content of approximately 83 g per meal, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should choose a wholegrain or multigrain bread option to reduce the glycemic impact, limit portion to half a sub, and avoid sugary beverages in the combo to manage postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combined high sodium, saturated fat from processed deli meats, and trans fat content pose compounded cardiovascular risk; the full meal delivers approximately 7.2 g saturated fat and over 2300 mg sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should treat this meal as an occasional indulgence only, selecting leaner protein options, requesting extra vegetables, omitting high-fat sauces, and choosing a wholegrain roll to partially mitigate saturated fat and refined carbohydrate burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Italian B.M.T. Soup Meal is a Subway fast-food combo meal sold in Singapore, consisting of a 6-inch Italian B.M.T. submarine sandwich paired with a cup of soup. The name 'B.M.T.' stands for 'Bigger, Meatier, Tastier' and the sandwich features a combination of three Italian-style deli meats — pepperoni, salami, and ham — layered on a soft Italian white or wheat sub roll with fresh vegetables including lettuce, tomato, onion, green pepper, olives, and pickles, finished with the customer's choice of sauce. The sandwich may be served toasted or untoasted. The soup accompaniment is typically a vegetable-based or tomato-based soup. The meal is hearty, savoury, and moderately filling, with a chewy bread texture contrasting with the tender deli meats and crisp vegetables. Nutritionally, this meal is high in sodium (approximately 2340 mg per serving) due to the cured and processed meats, and delivers a substantial carbohydrate load (~83 g per serving) primarily from the refined bread roll, resulting in a high glycemic load (GL ≈ 44). It provides moderate protein (~37 g per serving) and is a source of B vitamins, iron, and calcium. Clinically, this meal is of concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium, saturated fat, and refined carbohydrate content. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those on halal-certified diets unless specifically confirmed by the outlet. In Singapore, this meal is available at Subway outlets on field survey and is priced at approximately SGD 15.

confidence_reason: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists for this branded Subway combo meal. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Food data for the Subway Italian B.M.T. 6-inch sub and USDA SR Legacy vegetable soup, proportionally combined. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for white-bread-based submarine sandwiches. Confidence is medium because the source match is adapted rather than exact, and the soup component varies by outlet.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 4. Per-100 g values represent a weighted composite of the 6-inch Italian B.M.T. sub (~62% by weight, ~280 g) and vegetable soup (~38% by weight, ~170 g) for a total serving of 450 g. 5. Halal status is not confirmed for Subway Singapore outlets; consumers should verify directly with the outlet. 6. The soup variety may vary by outlet and season; nutritional values assume a standard vegetable soup. 7. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate based on white-bread submarine sandwich data from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008); no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. 8. Glycemic load per serving = (53 × 83.25) / 100 ≈ 44.0, based on 18.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 450 g serving.
